# Topic Page Template

Use this template for every core roadmap page so the atlas stays comparable across topics.

## In-page Navigation

- `Topic Thesis`
- `Why This Topic Matters`
- `Problem Decomposition`
- `Core Technical Routes`
- `Classical Backbone`
- `Frontier Watchlist (2025-2026)`
- `Open-source Projects & Toolchains`
- `Datasets / Benchmarks / Simulators`
- `Academic Labs & Company Systems`
- `Practical Build Paths`
- `Common Failure Modes`
- `Reading Sequence`
- `Open Questions`

## Required Curation Rules

- Lead with system placement, not paper chronology.
- Separate `classical backbone` from `frontier watchlist`.
- Prefer official project pages first, code second, papers third.
- Every listed item needs a one-line judgment explaining why it matters.
- Mark which items are `beginner-friendly`, `reproducible`, `industrial signal`, or `frontier-only` where useful.
- Reuse the shared labels `classical`, `frontier-2025-2026`, `open-source`, `benchmark`, `simulator`, `industry-system`, and `build-path`.

## Minimal Item Counts

- `Classical Backbone`: 5-8
- `Frontier Watchlist`: 8-15
- `Open-source Projects & Toolchains`: 5-10
- `Datasets / Benchmarks / Simulators`: 3-6
- `Academic Labs & Company Systems`: 5-10
- `Practical Build Paths`: 2-4

## Writing Standard

- Write for readers who want to build, not only browse.
- Use tables when comparison matters.
- Add cross-links to adjacent roadmap, resource, map, and build-path pages.
- End with what the reader should do next.
